title: Tanker report - Week 6
summary:
VLCC
The Chinese Lunar New Year Holiday has kept this sector quiet this week. In the Middle East region, there was no shift in the assessment of 280,000mt to US Gulf via the Cape/Cape routing at WS18/18.5 level and the 270,000mt to China market is now rated at WS31.5 - a modest rise of a fraction of a point. In the Atlantic arena, rates for west Africa to China slipped a point to WS32.5, while from US Gulf to China rates eased about $12,000 to $4.225m lump sum.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The Chinese Lunar New Year Holiday has kept this sector quiet this week. In the Middle East region, there was no shift in the assessment of 280,000mt to US Gulf via the Cape/Cape routing at WS18/18.5 level and the 270,000mt to China market is now rated at WS31.5 - a modest rise of a fraction of a point.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Black Sea/Med rates have settled at last week's level between WS67.5 & 70 for 135,000mt, while the 130,000mt Nigeria/UK Continent market remains in the doldrums in the low WS50s. Basrah/Med fell back three points to about WS18-19 for 135,000mt.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The cross Med market of Ceyhan/Lavera slipped a point to WS75. However, at the time of writing, there is a slightly firmer feel.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: It has been a rather lacklustre and flat week for most routes. Middle East Gulf/Japan LR2 rates generally hovered in the low to mid WS60s basis 75,000mt, whilst the LR1s had a slow week with rates easing 2.5 points to WS80 level.
